metadata.conformance_note: torch.mps is Apple-Silicon-specific. On Linux/WSL the backend is always unavailable. Fixtures record device-detection and lifecycle behaviour which is meaningful on all platforms.

fixtures.platform_note: always False on non-Apple platforms; always False in ferrotorch stub
always 0 on non-Apple platforms; always 0 in ferrotorch stub

fixtures.note: torch.device('mps') construction is always valid; ferrotorch MpsDevice::new always returns Err(DeviceUnavailable) until #451
Same as mps_device_count(); both must return 0 on non-MPS platforms
ordinal() round-trips the value from ::new; test is structural (via Display) since ::new always errors
ferrotorch init_mps_backend always returns Err(DeviceUnavailable) until #451; torch has no direct equivalent — synchronize() would raise on non-MPS
ferrotorch Display mirrors torch.mps device ordinal in 'mps:N' form
